introSection: This was a steel roller coaster that previously operated at Chongqing Amusement Park in Nan'an, Chongqing, China. It operated from February 1991 to 2006.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride was originally built as a standard Jet Spiral Coaster at the Shenzhen Bay Hotel in Nanshan, Shenzhen, Guangdong, China, which used to have a double corkscrew element and the overall layout of the model. It closed at an unknown point in time, however, it re-emerged at Chongqing Amusement Park, opening in February 1991. In 1997, the double corkscrew was replaced by a single vertical loop. The ride was removed in 2006 and replaced by the Roller Coaster.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: It originally had a double corkscrew element.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 6 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 24 riders per train.

fullText:
This was a steel roller coaster that previously operated at Chongqing Amusement Park in Nan'an, Chongqing, China. It operated from February 1991 to 2006.

The ride was originally built as a standard Jet Spiral Coaster at the Shenzhen Bay Hotel in Nanshan, Shenzhen, Guangdong, China, which used to have a double corkscrew element and the overall layout of the model. It closed at an unknown point in time, however, it re-emerged at Chongqing Amusement Park, opening in February 1991. In 1997, the double corkscrew was replaced by a single vertical loop. The ride was removed in 2006 and replaced by the Roller Coaster.

It originally had a double corkscrew element.

White track and supports.

6 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 24 riders per train.
